channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Italy takes back 43 migrants it planned to process in Albania, a 3rd failed attempt to keep them out
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship on Saturday took migrants to Italy from asylum processing centers in Albania following a court decision in Rome. It was the third failed attempt by Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government to process migrants in the non-EU country.
item.dc:creator: ERION XHABAFTI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas frees 3 hostages and Israel releases Palestinian prisoners in 4th exchange of ceasefire
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas militants freed three male hostages held for more than a year in the Gaza Strip on Saturday and Israel released 183 Palestinian prisoners from Israeli jails in the fourth such exchange of a ceasefire deal that has halted 15 months of intense fighting.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian drone and missile attacks kill 8 in Ukraine
item.description: At least eight people died overnight as Russian drone and missile strikes pounded Ukraine’s towns and cities and Moscow’s troops continued their grinding advance through the country’s east.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump uniquely placed to ‘whisper’ in Erdogan’s ear over Turkish regional ambitions: Greek defense minister
item.description: Greek Defense Minister Nikos Dendias said NATO needs to take seriously Turkey's expansionist ambitions and President Erdoğan's desire to "recreate the Ottoman Empire" amid aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases 3 more hostages, including US citizen, as part of fragile ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas on Saturday freed three more hostages, including American-Israeli Keith Siegel, in the latest phase of a fragile ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels move deeper into eastern Congo as UN reports executions and rapes
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels were quickly expanding their presence in eastern Congo after capturing Goma, the region's major city, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between Maduro, Trump's envoy
item.description: Grenell’s hours long trip to Venezuela, according to the White House, was focused on Trump’s efforts to deport Venezuelans back to their home country.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 6 American hostages in Venezuela freed after Trump envoy meets with Maduro
item.description: Six Americans who were being held hostage in Venezuela were freed Friday night following President Trump’s envoy for special missions Richard Grenell's visit to the South American country. "Just been informed that we are bringing six hostages home from Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Polus, The Hill

item.title: Trump refuses to say if he’s spoken to Putin; White House ‘talking’ about ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump on Friday wouldn’t say if he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since he began his second term but said there are ongoing discussions about ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Serbia rocked by anti-corruption protests after construction tragedy
item.description: Tens of thousands of people in Serbia have been marching in the streets to protest alleged Serbian government corruption after an accident that killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Taliban take over Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel, more than a decade after attacking it
item.description: The Taliban are taking over the operations of Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel in Kabul, more than a decade after they launched a deadly attack there that killed nine people. The Serena Hotel said Friday it was closing its operations in the Afghan capital on Feb.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Grenell to give Venezuela a warning from Trump administration
item.description: President Trump’s envoy for special missions, Richard Grenell, is delivering an in-person message to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro that there will be consequences if detained Americans are not released and Venezuelan “criminals and gang members” deported from the U.S. are not repatriated.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Panama president: 'Impossible' to negotiate over canal
item.description: Panama President José Raúl Mulino rejected negotiations over ownership of the Panama Canal, in the face of President Trump’s fixation on the U.S. retaking the trade waterway.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Growing anger in Greece over deadly rail disaster prompts plan by opposition to challenge government
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Three Greek opposition parties vowed Friday to challenge the country’s center-right government with a censure motion over its handling of a deadly rail disaster nearly two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Expected release of high-profile male hostages sparks excitement and anxiety in Israel
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — News that three high-profile hostages are expected to be released Saturday, including the father of the youngest captives held in Gaza, brought excitement and trepidation to Israel on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lithuanian president backs Trump’s NATO defense spending goal amid ongoing Russian threat
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Securing an end to the fighting in Ukraine must take place with Kyiv's full involvement and come with more defense spending by regional countries to avoid any future Russian aggression, according to Lithuania's president.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands of Muslims attend annual Biswa Ijtema event in Bangladesh
item.description: TONGI, Bangladesh (AP) — Tens of thousands of people gathered on a riverbank near Bangladesh’s capital on Friday to listen to sermons by Islamic scholars at the Biswa Ijtema, or global congregation of Muslim devotees.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First American hostage to be released from Gaza, Hamas says
item.description: Keith Segel, an American-Israeli in Gaza and the first of American citizens taken on Oct. 7, will be released on Saturday as part of the Israel-Hamas ceasfire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and ISABEL DeBRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An Iraqi man who carried out Quran burnings in Sweden is killed in a shooting
item.description: Salwan Momika, a 38-year-old Iraqi man who staged several burnings and desecrations of the Quran in Sweden in 2023, has been killed in a shooting near Stockholm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caribbean nation of St Kitts launches investigation after 19 people found dead in drifting vessel
item.description: Authorities in the Caribbean nation of St. Kitts and Nevis have launched an investigation after discovering a partially submerged boat containing at least 19 bodies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu delays Palestinian prisoner release after 'chaotic' Hamas scenes
item.description: Israel will delay the release of Palestinian prisoners in Israeli jails amid chaotic scenes surrounding Hamas's release of hostages it kidnapped from Israel and held since its attack on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Chaos and uncertainty swirl around Trump's foreign aid freeze
item.description: President Trump’s decision to clean house at the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) and initially freeze all foreign assistance is fueling chaos and uncertainty in Washington and across the world.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Investigators recover black boxes from plane in DC crash
item.description: There were 60 passengers and four crew members aboard the plane, which was arriving from Wichita, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Woman used wigs, disguises to take British citizenship tests for at least 14 people, authorities say
item.description: A British woman used wigs and disguises to impersonate applicants taking citizenship tests across the United Kingdom, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Backlash may shatter President Macron's plans to leave mark on Notre Dame windows
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron’s plans for installing contemporary windows inside the restored Notre Dame Cathedral is facing furious opposition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Firecrackers popped, incense was offered at temples and dancers and drummers paraded Wednesday in Asia and farther afield as millions around the world celebrated the Lunar New Year.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At least 30 dead in stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India, police say
item.description: At least 30 people were killed and another 60 injured in a stampede at the Maha Kumbh festival, a six-week Hindu festival and the world’s largest religious gathering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denmark to increase military spending in North Atlantic by $2 billion amid row with Trump over Greenland
item.description: Denmark is increasing military spending in the North Atlantic amid President Donald Trump’s bid to have Greenland sold or ceded to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Holocaust survivor recalls mother's encounter with Nazi
item.description: Marianne Muller joins “NewsNation Prime” to share her family's story of courage and survival during the Holocaust.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean plane crash report says bird remains were found in engines, but no cause yet revealed
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The first report on last month’s Jeju Air crash in South Korea confirmed traces of bird strikes in the plane's engines, though officials haven’t determined the cause of the accident that killed all but two of the 181 people on board.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
